explanation: This command uses a direct string instead of base64 encoding to bypass the Sigma rule's detection of encoded WMI class names.
This command splits the WMI class name into parts and concatenates them, avoiding the base64-encoded string detection.
This command uses a mix of string literals and variables to construct the WMI class name, evading the encoded string check.
This command uses ASCII character codes to reconstruct the WMI class name, avoiding direct or encoded string detection.
This command combines a literal string with ASCII characters to form the WMI class name, bypassing the encoded string detection.
This command uses an environment variable to store the WMI class name, making it harder for the rule to detect the encoded or direct string.
This command encodes and then decodes the WMI class name using UTF-8 encoding, which is a safe mutation technique that avoids direct or base64-encoded string detection.
This command uses the String.Concat method to combine parts of the WMI class name, avoiding direct or encoded string detection.
